Were  her comments on the smug side?! absolutely but the hate was overblown. She's far from the worst in terms of politics and social issues. text: how did we go from super Mario bro's movie to a Mario galaxy movie. that's like going from iron man to infinity war in 2 movies. which is what the dceu did. they're skipping super Mario bros 2, which the whole movie could be a dream like in the game. super Mario bros 3 with the different worlds that the movie could take place in. the desert world, the world where everything is giant, the ice world, and the world of pipes. super Mario world brought in yoshi, 64 had him jumping into paintings, and hell, even super Mario sunshine had him on an island cleaning up pollution and fighting all kinds of hell. there is so much they are skipping and they're going straight into galaxy because it's the newer Mario stuff that the kiddies recognize. well, i guess they could do what they want since i don't really care to see it. i have the games.

author: @CrimsonSunriseProductions
text:
Even putting aside all the drama, Brie isn't the right casting.

Rosalina has a worldweary but loving, motherly tone in the games, painting the picture of a character who, despite missing her home and complacent with the universe resetting (as implied by Galaxy 1's finale), still has a loving heart and has the paitence of a saint while never patronising anyone.

Brie's voice is the the safest, most "I'm not like other girls" performance she has in ALL her performances, barring Room.

author: @Jockolantern
text:
I loathe what Nintendo has become.  Allowing the westernizing of Mario in movie form is abhorrent.  The first Illumination movie was awful, this sequel appears to be expanding Rosalina's role unnecessarily similar to Mario's role being deflated within the first movie by not allowing him to rescue the princess.  And Miyamoto gave this schlock his blessing.  Disgraceful.

It's all so tiresome.  Formulaic.  Lazy cashing in with normies and nostalgia-baiting OG fans while refusing to give us new additions to the 2D platforming we grew up loving or iterating on the 3D platforming which gave us masterpieces like the Galaxy games.  Why would I want to watch a movie when I can play the infinitely superior games?

Nintendo seems to have plenty of time to sue emulation sites and indie developers for providing the services and games Nintendo refuses to though.  So at least their priorities are where the belong...  Right?

This nonsense isn't getting a dime from me or, more importantly, my time.  Pathetic to see how much Nintendo has devolved and abandoned the core fans who made them this successful.

author: @maverick77defender
text:
“Captain Marvel” producer Jonathan Schwartz, who also produced the “Guardians of the Galaxy” movies, calls Brie Larson the "next Tom Cruise."

“On any other Marvel movie, stunt doubles would be doing 99 percent of what she’s doing,” he said on the set. She spent so much time at the stunt gym, "we sort of had to say, ‘You need to get some rest. Stop pushing yourself so hard.’

"But when you see this movie, the amount of practical stunts that she’s doing by herself will boggle your mind, and make you say, ‘I can’t believe they let her do that.’ ”

author: @istjmoneymaker
text:
I don't think we should hold a grudge over past differences, for a voice acting role I don't really care all that much, the animation does all the work. Was anya taylor the best peach not really, but it didn't ruin that movie, I don't think it will ruin this one either, if this means they're going to somehow inject politics into the movie maybe, but she is a big nintendo fan, so I don't begrudge her filling this role. 

The first captain marvel movie wasn't that bad, I liked it more than some of the marvel movies we've gotten, and is brie a great actress, she's not the best, but a voice acting role is different, you don't have to put your expressions and mannerisms on camera, so her performance her probably is a lot better, especially since, she probably understands the character of rosalina better, captain marvel just isn't really that relateable of a character, no one knows who that is. It was never a popular character, but there's a lot of rosalina fans, that's a cool one.

As far as grace randolph, just trying to slowly back away from paying attention to what she says, her takes have been less and less relateable, I tend to go against the grain and will defend someone everyone bashes, for a while I thought she was over hated and enjoyed her analysis, but lately its just been getting worse. She struggles to understand how popular some of this stuff is and how much of a sin it is to bash it for no genuine reason.
